title: What Should An AI Device Look Like? — With Alex Himel
summary: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, argues that smart glasses represent the next major computing platform and that AI assistants can enhance daily tasks, meetings, and fitness without disrupting user attention. The discussion covers Meta's Ray-Ban glasses strategy, competition from OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon, and unresolved challenges around on-device AI, privacy, and facial recognition.
writeup: In this conversation with Alex Kantrowitz, Meta's VP of Wearables Alex Himel makes the case that smart glasses are the next major computing platform, with AI assistants enhancing daily tasks, meetings, and fitness while preserving user attention. The discussion details Meta's Ray-Ban glasses strategy and positions it against competition from OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon. Unresolved challenges get attention as well: on-device AI, privacy, and facial recognition.
